name: Watchtowers and coves of Formentera — circular trailrunning from Pilar de la Mola
name_original: Miradores y calas de Formentera — circular trailrunning desde Pilar de la Mola
region_intro: Jogging around Formentera offers diverse terrain, from flat coastal paths to more elevated areas like La Mola. The island features white sandy beaches, fragrant pine and juniper forests, and dirt roads winding through rural landscapes. Runners can explore signposted green routes, coastal stretches, and the unique environment of Ses Salines Natural Park, which includes salt lakes and rich biodiversity. Best jogging routes around Formentera The most popular jogging route is Watchtowers and coves of Formentera — circular trailrunning from Pilar de la Mola, a 6.5 miles (10.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 18 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the island's watchtowers and coves. Another top favourite among local runners is Salt Lagoon – Estany Pudent (Stinking Lake) loop from es Pujols, a moderate 7.1 miles (11.5 km) path. This route circles the Salt Lagoon, providing scenic views of the water and surrounding landscape. Local runners also love the Ses Illetes Beach loop from la Savina, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail leading through coastal areas and past the famous Ses Illetes Beach, often completed in about 55 minutes. Jogging around Formentera is defined by white sandy beaches, pine forests, and salt lakes. The network of routes offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal runs to more challenging trails with moderate elevation. The routes in Formentera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 80 reviews. More than 2600 runners have used komoot to explore Formentera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Formentera?
A: Formentera offers a wide variety of running experiences, with over 230 routes available. These include 28 easy routes, 178 moderate routes, and 32 more challenging trails, catering to all fitness levels.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on Formentera's jogging paths?
A: Formentera's jogging paths feature diverse terrain, from largely flat coastal stretches with white sandy beaches and crystal-clear waters to paths through fragrant pine and juniper forests. You'll also find dirt roads winding past traditional white houses and cultivated fields. The eastern and western plateaus offer more elevated areas with dramatic cliff coasts and iconic lighthouses.

Q: When is the best time of year for running in Formentera?
A: The mild climate of Formentera makes it suitable for running for much of the year. To avoid the peak heat, especially during summer months, it's best to plan your runs for early mornings or late afternoons. Spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or natural sights to see while running in Formentera?
A: Yes, many routes offer stunning views and pass by significant landmarks. You can run past iconic lighthouses like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/601057" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2995920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbària Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, explore the unique environment of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5389257" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Salt Flats of Ibiza&lt;/a&gt; within Ses Salines Natural Park, or discover natural formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/730372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foradada Cave&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular running routes in Formentera?
A: Yes, Formentera has several excellent circular running routes. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000813659/watchtowers-and-coves-of-formentera-circular-trailrunning-from-pilar-de-la-mola" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Watchtowers and coves of Formentera — circular trailrunning from Pilar de la Mola&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 10.5 km (6.5 miles) trail offering scenic views. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14681187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt Lagoon – Estany Pudent (Stinking Lake) loop from es Pujols&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11.5 km (7.1 miles) and circles the picturesque lagoon.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Formentera?
A: The running routes in Formentera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 80 reviews. Runners often praise the island's diverse terrain, from beautiful beaches to pine forests and salt lakes, and the well-signposted 'green routes' that make exploration easy. Over 2600 runners have used komoot to discover Formentera's varied landscapes.

Q: Can I find running routes that offer coastal views or beach access?
A: Absolutely. Formentera is renowned for its stunning coastline. Many routes provide breathtaking views of white sandy beaches and turquoise waters. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21937960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ses Illetes Beach loop from la Savina&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, leading through coastal areas and past the famous Ses Illetes Beach. You can also find routes near Cala Saona and Platja de Migjorn.

Q: Are there family-friendly running trails in Formentera?
A: Formentera offers many accessible paths suitable for families. The island's largely flat terrain, especially along the coast and through its interior dirt roads, provides easy options. Look for the signposted 'green routes' which are generally stable and well-suited for exploring on foot with children.

Q: Are there dog-friendly running trails in Formentera?
A: Many of Formentera's natural paths and dirt roads are suitable for running with dogs, especially in the quieter rural interiors and along less crowded coastal stretches. It's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, particularly in protected areas like Ses Salines Natural Park, and to be mindful of local regulations and other trail users.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by Ses Platjetes?
A: Yes, you can find routes that incorporate the beautiful area of Ses Platjetes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38244754" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Saona Beach – Ses Platjetes (The Little Beaches) loop from H.RIU LA MOLA PLATJA MIGJORN&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11.6 km (7.2 miles) route that allows you to explore this scenic coastal area.

Q: What are the options for easy jogging routes in Formentera?
A: Formentera has 28 easy jogging routes, many of which are found along the flat coastal paths or through the island's gentle rural interiors. These routes are perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed run, allowing you to enjoy the scenery without significant elevation changes.

Q: Can I access running trails using public transport or find parking nearby?
A: Formentera has a network of public transport that can take you to various starting points for trails, especially in main towns like La Savina or Es Pujols. Many trailheads, particularly those outside of urban centers, also have parking available, making it convenient to access routes by car. It's recommended to check specific route details for parking information.
tips: Great view from the "Roman road" on the coast of Formentera.
Small cove located to the south of the island of Formentera, between the beaches of Migjorn and Es Ram. It is located at the eastern end of the Migjorn beach, where the Mola cliffs begin.

waypoints.name: View of the Coast of Formentera from the Roman Road
